records.ety: [AS. gyldan, from gold gold. √234. See Gold.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Gilded or Gilt (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Gilding.]

defs: To overlay with a thin covering of gold; to cover with a golden color; to cause to look like gold.
quotes: No more the rising sun shall gild the morn.

defs: To make attractive; to adorn; to brighten.
quotes: Let oft good humor, mild and gay, Gild the calm evening of your day.

defs: To give a fair but deceptive outward appearance to; to embellish; as, to gild a lie.

quotes: This grand liquior that hath gilded them.
